456\n\n                   Statement of Conclusions ................................................................. 457\n                   Disposition .......................................................................................... 474\n\n\n                   PREFACE\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_3\">                   1.       This batch of writ petitions instituted by individual Chartered\n\n\n\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_1\">                   W.P.(C) 1065/2021 &amp; other connected matters                                                    Page 8 of 476</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_2\">Signature Not Verified</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_3\">Digitally Signed</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_4\">By:KAMLESH KUMAR</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_5\">Signing Date:07.02.2025</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_6\">18:46:26</span>\n                    Accountants 1 as well as auditing firms assail the validity of <a href=\"/doc/102897483/\" id=\"a_3\">Section\n                   132(4)</a> of the Companies Act, 2013 2. A challenge is additionally raised\n                   to Rules 3, 8, 10 and 11 of the National Financial Reporting\n                   Authority Rules, 20183.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"PetArg\" id=\"p_4\">                   2.      For the purposes of the challenge which stands raised to the\n                   statutory provisions aforenoted, the petitioners seek a declaration that\n                   those provisions be struck down as being unconstitutional on the\n                   ground of being arbitrary and ultra vires. In the alternative, the\n                   petitioners seek an appropriate declaration to the effect that <a href=\"/doc/455488/\" id=\"a_4\">Section\n                   132(4)</a> of the Companies Act as well as Rules 3, 8 10 and 11 of the\n                   NFRA Rules be held not to apply to any audit completed before 01\n                   October 2018. The prayer in the alternative essentially calls upon the\n                   Court to read down those provisions as being inapplicable to audits that\n                   may have been completed prior to the introduction of Section 132 in the\n                   <a href=\"/doc/1353758/\" id=\"a_5\">Companies Act</a> and thus avoid a declaration of invalidity being\n                   rendered.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_5\">                   3.      The petitioners in this batch have individually impugned the\n                   notices issued by the National Financial Regulatory Authority 4 in\n                   terms of which proceedings were sought to be initiated by the\n                   respondent for commencement of disciplinary action in respect of\n                   perceived acts of &quot;professional or other misconduct&quot; and for\n                   consequential imposition of penalties. In some of the writ petitions,\n                   final orders of punishment including that of debarment had also come\n                   to be passed and which too have been impugned in the instant writ\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_7\">                   1</span>\n                     CAs\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_8\">                   2</span>\n                     <a href=\"/doc/1353758/\" id=\"a_6\">Companies Act</a>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_9\">                   3</span>\n                     NFRA Rules\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_10\">                   4</span>\n                     NFRA\n\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_11\">                   W.P.(C) 1065/2021 &amp; other connected matters                   Page 9 of 476</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_12\">Signature Not Verified</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_13\">Digitally Signed</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_14\">By:KAMLESH KUMAR</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_15\">Signing Date:07.02.2025</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_16\">18:46:26</span>\n                    petitions.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_6\">                   4.        The challenge on the basis of constitutional invalidity is founded\n                   on a retroactive operation of <a href=\"/doc/455488/\" id=\"a_7\">Section 132</a> of the Companies Act and\n                   which is viewed as empowering the NFRA to initiate disciplinary\n                   proceedings not just against individual partners and CAs&#x27; but also\n                   auditing firms in respect of any audit that may have been conducted\n                   including those commenced and concluded prior to the introduction of\n                   that provision in the <a href=\"/doc/1353758/\" id=\"a_8\">Companies Act</a>.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Facts\" id=\"p_7\">                   5.        <a href=\"/doc/455488/\" id=\"a_9\">Section 132</a> came to be incorporated in the <a href=\"/doc/1353758/\" id=\"a_10\">Companies Act</a> by\n                   virtue of Act 18 of 2013 and came to be operationalized in terms of a\n                   notification issued on 01 October 2018. In terms of that notification, the\n                   Union Government designated the said date as the point of\n                   commencement as well as the date of constitution of the NFRA. It is in\n                   the aforesaid backdrop that the petitioners contend that <a href=\"/doc/455488/\" id=\"a_11\">Section 132</a> and\n                   its retrospective operation would be rendered unconstitutional and\n                   invalid since it contemplates the imposition of penalties as well as\n                   disciplinary action in terms which were not contemplated by the statute\n                   prior to 01 October 2018. The challenge to <a href=\"/doc/455488/\" id=\"a_12\">Section 132</a> also proceeds\n                   on the ground of an alleged lack of procedural due process as well as\n                   the deprivation of rights and safeguards which were conferred upon a\n                   CA or a firm while facing disciplinary action under the <a href=\"/doc/1826871/\" id=\"a_13\">Chartered\n                   Accountants Act, 1949</a> 5 read along with the Chartered Accountants\n                   Regulations,           1988 6       and       the   Chartered     Accountants\n                   (Procedure of Investigations of                Professional     and        Other\n\n\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_17\">                   5</span>\n                       <a href=\"/doc/1826871/\" id=\"a_14\">CA Act</a>.\n</p><span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_18\">                   6</span>\n<p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_8\">                       1988 Regulations\n\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_19\">                   W.P.(C) 1065/2021 &amp; other connected matters                           Page 10 of 476</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_20\">Signature Not Verified</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_21\">Digitally Signed</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_22\">By:KAMLESH KUMAR</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_23\">Signing Date:07.02.2025</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_24\">18:46:26</span>\n                    Misconduct and Conduct of Cases) Rules, 20077.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"PetArg\" id=\"p_9\">                   6.        The action of the respondents which stands impugned in this\n                   batch is also based on the various provisions contained in <a href=\"/doc/455488/\" id=\"a_15\">Section 132</a>\n                   itself as well as the NFRA Rules. This facet of the challenge is based on\n                   the petitioners arguing that in terms of the statutory provisions\n                   aforenoted, the NFRA is envisaged to discharge its functions and duties\n                   through various independent and separate &quot;divisions&quot;. According to the\n                   writ petitioners, the statute itself envisages the functions of monitoring\n                   and enforcement of accounting standards, overseeing quality of service,\n                   suggesting measures, the power to investigate as well as to undertake\n                   disciplinary action is contemplated to be discharged by separate\n                   divisions of the NFRA. According to the writ petitioners, in the facts of\n                   these cases it is apparent that the body which oversaw the audits in\n                   question was the same which came to the conclusion that there was a\n                   failure to comply with accounting standards itself and initiated\n                   proceedings for taking disciplinary action. They thus contend that the\n                   very same body that had drawn a report on the basis of which the\n                   disciplinary proceedings are sought to be initiated and undertaken has\n                   essentially donned the role of both prosecutor and judge. This,\n                   according to the petitioners, is sufficient to hold the initiation of action\n                   as being in clear violation of fair and due process and in breach of\n                   <a href=\"/doc/367586/\" id=\"a_16\">Article 14</a> of the Constitution.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_10\">                   7.        While we had concluded the hearing on this batch of writ\n                   petitions on 22 July 2024, the pronouncement of the present judgment\n                   was delayed primarily on account of written submissions being\n\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_25\">                   7</span>\n                       Rules 2007.\n</p>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_26\">                   W.P.(C) 1065/2021 &amp; other connected matters                     Page 11 of 476</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_27\">Signature Not Verified</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_28\">Digitally Signed</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_29\">By:KAMLESH KUMAR</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_30\">Signing Date:07.02.2025</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_31\">18:46:26</span>\n<p data-structure=\"PetArg\" id=\"p_11\">                    tendered by NFRA only on 06 October 2024. The Court was thereafter\n                   constrained to await any response that the petitioners may have wished\n                   to submit since those submissions were circulated amongst learned\n                   counsels for the petitioners only around that time.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_12\">                   DISCIPLINARY PROCEDURE AS PER THE CA ACT\n</p><p data-structure=\"Facts\" id=\"p_13\">                   8.      Having noticed the principal grounds of challenge, it becomes\n                   appropriate to note that prior to the introduction of <a href=\"/doc/455488/\" id=\"a_17\">Section 132</a> of the\n                   2013 Act, disciplinary proceedings against a CA was regulated by the\n                   provisions contained in the <a href=\"/doc/1826871/\" id=\"a_18\">CA Act</a> read along with the 1988\n                   Regulations and the Misconduct Rules 2007. <a href=\"/doc/1826871/\" id=\"a_19\">The CA Act</a> incorporated\n                   provisions in Chapter V for the purposes of undertaking disciplinary\n                   proceedings against a member or a firm. Prior to certain amendments\n                   which were introduced in the <a href=\"/doc/1826871/\" id=\"a_20\">CA Act</a> in 2006 and 2022, disciplinary\n                   proceedings against a member of a firm were to be undertaken by a\n                   Disciplinary Directorate which was a body charged with undertaking a\n                   preliminary examination of a complaint, a Board of Discipline which\n                   was to try cases of professional or other misconduct specified in the\n                   <a href=\"/doc/1826871/\" id=\"a_21\">First Schedule of the CA Act</a> and the Disciplinary Committee in respect\n                   of members found to be guilty of professional or other misconduct\n                   mentioned in either the Second Schedule or both the First and the\n                   Second Schedules forming part of the <a href=\"/doc/1826871/\" id=\"a_22\">CA Act, 1949</a>.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_14\">                   9.      By virtue of amendments which came to be ushered by Act 12 of\n                   2022, various amendments came to be introduced in <a href=\"/doc/1060361/\" id=\"a_23\">Sections 2</a> which\n                   also and alongside saw the introduction of <a href=\"/doc/116448511/\" id=\"a_24\">Sections 21A</a> and <a href=\"/doc/114878287/\" id=\"a_25\">21B</a> in the\n                   <a href=\"/doc/1826871/\" id=\"a_26\">CA Act</a>. Act 12 of 2022 also introduced provisions pertaining to the\n                   registration of firms. As the provisions stood in the <a href=\"/doc/1826871/\" id=\"a_27\">CA Act</a> around the\n\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_32\">                   W.P.(C) 1065/2021 &amp; other connected matters                  Page 12 of 476</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_33\">Signature Not Verified</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_34\">Digitally Signed</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_35\">By:KAMLESH KUMAR</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_36\">Signing Date:07.02.2025</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_37\">18:46:26</span>\n                    time when Section 132 came to be introduced, the Council as\n                   contemplated under <a href=\"/doc/1065170/\" id=\"a_28\">Section 9</a> of the CA Act, stood empowered to\n                   establish a Disciplinary Directorate headed by an officer designated as\n                   the Director Discipline for undertaking investigations in respect of any\n                   information or complaint received by the Council. In terms of <a href=\"/doc/1248650/\" id=\"a_29\">Section\n                   21</a>, the Director Discipline was firstly charged with analyzing the\n                   information or complaint and arrive at a prima facie opinion with\n                   respect to the alleged misconduct. Wherever a Director Discipline were\n                   to form the opinion that a member was guilty of any professional or\n                   other misconduct specified in the First Schedule, it was required to\n                   place the matter before the Board of Discipline. If the Director\n                   Discipline were to be of the opinion that the misconduct would be\n                   classifiable either in the Second Schedule or both the Schedules, it was\n                   obliged to place the matter before the Disciplinary Committee.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_15\">                   10.     The Board of Discipline by virtue of <a href=\"/doc/116448511/\" id=\"a_30\">Section 21A</a> of the CA Act\n                   was to comprise of persons with experience in law and having\n                   knowledge of disciplinary matters, two members of which one would\n                   be a member of the Council elected by the said body itself and the other\n                   being a member nominated by the Union Government from amongst\n                   persons of eminence having experience in the field of law, economics,\n                   business, finance or accountancy. The Director Discipline in terms of\n                   <a href=\"/doc/116448511/\" id=\"a_31\">Section 21A</a> was to function as the Secretary of the Board of Discipline.\n                   By virtue of <a href=\"/doc/4277052/\" id=\"a_32\">Section 21A(3)</a>, the Board of Discipline upon coming to\n                   the conclusion that a member was guilty of professional or other\n                   misconduct mentioned in the First Schedule, could impose one or more\n                   of the following punishments:-\n</p>\n\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_38\">                   W.P.(C) 1065/2021 &amp; other connected matters                  Page 13 of 476</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_39\">Signature Not Verified</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_40\">Digitally Signed</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_41\">By:KAMLESH KUMAR</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_42\">Signing Date:07.02.2025</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_43\">18:46:26</span>\n<pre id=\"pre_2\">                           A.    Reprimand the member\n\n                          B.    Remove the name of the member from the Register\n\n                          C.    Impose such fine as it may think fit subject to the prescription\n                                of the fine not exceeding INR 1 lakh.\n\n</pre><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_16\">                   11.     The Disciplinary Committee in terms of <a href=\"/doc/114878287/\" id=\"a_33\">Section 21B</a> was\n                   comprised of the President or the Vice President of the Council to act as\n                   the Presiding Officer and two members to be elected from amongst the\n                   members of the Council. The Disciplinary Committee was also\n                   envisaged to comprise of two members nominated by the Union\n                   Government from amongst persons of eminence having experience in\n                   the field of law, economics, business, finance or accountancy. It thus\n                   became a five-member body which upon coming to the conclusion that\n                   a member was guilty of professional or a misconduct mentioned in the\n                   Second Schedule or both the Schedules could proceed to take any one\n                   or more of the following actions namely:-\n</p>\n<pre id=\"pre_3\">                          A.    Reprimand the member\n\n                          B.    Remove the name of the member from the Register\n</pre><p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_17\">                                permanently or for such period as it may think fit\n\n                          C.    Impose such fine as deemed appropriate and which could\n                                extend to INR 5 lakhs.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Facts\" id=\"p_18\">                   12.     Post promulgation of Act 12 of 2022, the Director Discipline\n                   came to be empowered by statute to undertake an investigation either\n                   suo moto or on receipt of information or complaint. In the course of\n                   undertaking that investigation, where the Director Discipline were to\n                   find that the case merited further inquiry, it was obliged to provide an\n\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_44\">                   W.P.(C) 1065/2021 &amp; other connected matters                       Page 14 of 476</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_45\">Signature Not Verified</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_46\">Digitally Signed</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_47\">By:KAMLESH KUMAR</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_48\">Signing Date:07.02.2025</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_49\">18:46:26</span>\n                    opportunity to the member or the firm as the case may be, to submit a\n                   written statement. Upon the Director Discipline finding prima facie that\n                   a case for professional or other misconduct mentioned in the First\n                   Schedule was made out, it was required to submit a preliminary\n                   examination report to the Board of Discipline. Similarly, if it came to\n                   the prima facie conclusion that a professional or other misconduct was\n                   found to be established and would fall either in the Second Schedule or\n                   both the Schedules, it was required to transmit its preliminary\n                   examination report to the Disciplinary Committee.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_19\">                   13.     <a href=\"/doc/1248650/\" id=\"a_34\">Section 21</a> further contemplated that if a Director Discipline\n                   were to come to form the opinion that no prima facie case was made\n                   out either against the member or the firm, it was required to transmit\n                   the complaint along with all relevant documents to the Board of\n                   Discipline and if that body were to concur with the findings of the\n                   Director Discipline, it could direct a closure of the proceedings. If it\n                   were to disagree and continue action upon the complaint itself, it could\n                   refer the matter either to the Disciplinary Committee or even advise the\n                   Director Discipline to undertake a further investigation. <a href=\"/doc/116448511/\" id=\"a_35\">Sections 21A</a>\n                   and <a href=\"/doc/114878287/\" id=\"a_36\">21B</a> thus encapsulated the procedure which was liable to be\n                   adhered to by the Board of Discipline and the Disciplinary Committee\n                   while exercising their respective powers to undertake disciplinary\n                   action against a member of the firm.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_20\">                   14.     Professional or other misconduct was defined in <a href=\"/doc/784882/\" id=\"a_37\">Section 22</a> of\n                   the CA Act which in turn bids one to proceed to the First and the\n                   Second Schedules which set out the various acts or omissions which\n                   would amount to misconduct. For purposes of lucidity, we deem it\n\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_50\">                   W.P.(C) 1065/2021 &amp; other connected matters                  Page 15 of 476</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_51\">Signature Not Verified</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_52\">Digitally Signed</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_53\">By:KAMLESH KUMAR</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_54\">Signing Date:07.02.2025</span>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_55\">18:46:26</span>\n                    appropriate to extract <a href=\"/doc/1248650/\" id=\"a_38\">Sections 21</a>, <a href=\"/doc/116448511/\" id=\"a_39\">21A</a> and <a href=\"/doc/114878287/\" id=\"a_40\">21B</a> in the form of a\n                   comparative table and which captures how those set of provisions read\n                   prior to and after their substitution by Act 12 of 2022:-\n</p>\n<pre id=\"pre_4\">                          Section Pre-Amendment                           Post-Amendment\n\n                          <a href=\"/doc/1248650/\" id=\"a_41\">S. 21</a>   <a href=\"/doc/1248650/\" id=\"a_42\">21</a>.                  15.     At this juncture we deem it apposite to note that even though the\n                   amendments came to be introduced vide <a href=\"/doc/1248650/\" id=\"a_49\">Sections 21</a>, <a href=\"/doc/784882/\" id=\"a_50\">22</a> and <a href=\"/doc/1980358/\" id=\"a_51\">23</a> of the\n                   Act No. 12 of 2022, however the same have not been notified till date.\n                   In this regard, reference may be made to Notification dated 10 May\n                   2022 and which reads as under:-\n</p>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_14\">                            &quot;S.O. 2184(E).--In exercise of the powers conferred by sub-\n                            section (2) of section 1 of the Chartered Accountants, the Cost and\n                            Works Accountants and the Company Secretaries (Amendment)\n                            Act, 2022 (12 of 2022), the Central Government hereby appoints\n                            the 10th day of May, 2022, as the date on which the following\n                            provisions of the said Act shall come into force, namely:-\n</blockquote><blockquote id=\"blockquote_15\">                               SI.
